zh.Bryn, Glamorgan, Wales: 英國威爾斯格拉莫爾根布萊恩
zh.lifeSummary: Mrs Thomas Bevon ap Leyson於1325年出生在威爾士格拉莫爾根布萊恩。她和Thomas Bevon ap Leyson of Glamorgan至少有1個女兒。她去世在英國威爾斯格拉莫爾根布萊恩。

nameMeanings.name: Bevon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from the name of the county of Devon, which originates from an ancient British tribal name, Latin Dumnonii, perhaps meaning ‘worshipers of the god Dumnonos’. Compare Devonshire .

variants.name: Devenish
meanings: English: habitational name from Anglo-Norman French Deveneis, Middle English Devenish ‘of or from Devon’. See also Devon and Devonshire , compare Devonish .

variants.name: Devonshire
meanings: English: habitational name from the county of Devon, also known as Devonshire (see   Devon  ). Compare   Devonish  .

nameMeanings.name: ap
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Dutch: occupational name for a cobbler.
Slovenian: nickname for a talkative person, from lapati ‘to babble’, or a topographic name from dialect lap ‘abyss’. Compare Lapp 4.

variants.name: Lapp
meanings: German, English, and Dutch: from Middle High German and Middle English lappe, Middle Dutch lap ‘cloth, patch, rag’, a metonymic occupational name for a mender of clothes or shoes, or a nickname given to someone who wore ragged clothes or garments with loose folds, flaps, or hanging sleeves, or who had fleshy folds of skin on his face, neck, or body.

German: nickname for a simple-minded person, from Middle Low German lappe ‘fool’. This surname (in any of the two possible senses; see also 1 above) is also found in France (mainly Alsace, also Lorraine).
English: nickname from Anglo-Norman French lappe ‘burdock’, perhaps for someone with a prickly character.

nameMeanings.name: Leyson
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Leyshon
meanings: Welsh (Glamorgan): from the traditional Welsh male personal name Lleision.

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

en.lifeSummary: Mrs Thomas Bevon ap Leyson was born in 1325, in Bryn, Glamorgan, Wales. She had at least 1 daughter with Thomas Bevon ap Leyson of Glamorgan. She died in Bryn, Glamorgan, Wales, United Kingdom.
